name: Upama Shree Mahato
tagline: Machine Learning Engineer | M.S. in Electrical & Computer Engineering, NDSU
summary: ML Engineer with 3+ years of experience building production-grade ML systems end-to-end. Combines hands-on industry experience at Fusemachines across fraud detection, scalable ETL pipelines, and AWS deployment with graduate research at NDSU in computer vision, distributed deep learning, and model optimization. Experienced across the full ML lifecycle: data curation, model training, evaluation, and deployment.

education.degree: Master's in Electrical and Computer Engineering — GPA 3.78

responsibilities: Designed and benchmarked U-Net and its variants (UNet-VGG16, Deep VGG16+UNet, Attention U-Net with VGG16-BN) for pixel-wise semantic segmentation on 550 high-resolution UAV images (5472×3648 px) of sugar beet fields; best model achieved Dice 0.71 / IoU 0.59 at 1024×1024 patch resolution across 4 loss functions with systematic hyperparameter evaluation.

Implemented PyTorch FSDP distributed training across 2 GPUs with gradient accumulation (effective batch size 16), reducing per-epoch training time by 39%; deployed on NDSU CCAST HPC cluster using Singularity containers.

Engineered a hybrid memory optimization strategy (activation checkpointing + selective CPU offloading) to overcome a 150–170 GB activation memory bottleneck, enabling full-resolution training on a single NVIDIA H100 GPU.
Built a semi-automated annotation pipeline using Meta's Segment Anything Model (SAM) to generate pixel-level ground truth masks for 5 weed classes across the full dataset.
Designed a knowledge distillation framework (SqueezeNet → TinyCNN, 97k params / 0.37 MB) achieving 100% validation accuracy at 7× model compression for edge deployment.
Published and presented at the ASABE 2025 Annual International Meeting; delivered knowledge-transfer sessions to research team members.

Developed and evaluated fraud detection models (XGBoost, autoencoders, neural networks) on large-scale transactional data using PyTorch and Scikit-learn, achieving up to 80% detection performance with reduced false positives.
Built scalable Python and PySpark ETL pipelines for high-volume data ingestion, transformation, and feature engineering, cutting preprocessing time by 40% through automation.
Deployed and monitored ML workflows on AWS (EC2, S3); maintained production reliability through systematic debugging, pipeline optimization, and model monitoring.
Designed SQL reporting pipelines for large-scale data extraction and metric tracking; resolved data quality issues (missing values, class imbalance, outliers) through systematic preprocessing.
Collaborated with cross-functional engineering and business teams to translate requirements into data-driven solutions; documented all workflows for team-wide reproducibility.
Automated preprocessing and feature engineering on the IEEE-CIS fraud dataset; benchmarked ensemble models (SVM, XGBoost, autoencoders) and established reusable, documented experiment pipelines.
Led an NLP-based misinformation detection project using transformer-based classification (Hugging Face), delivering measurable accuracy improvements over baselines.
Built and deployed RESTful APIs using FastAPI and PostgreSQL with clean OOP design; implemented full CRUD operations, backend-to-frontend integration, and documented all schemas for team use.

publications.title: Accurate Pixel-Wise Object Detection Framework for Aerial Imagery
publications.authors: Mahato, U., Goli, N., Tida, U.R., Kim, J.Y.
publications.details: Developed a pixel-wise segmentation framework for high-resolution UAV imagery using U-Net and its variants with distributed training and memory optimization techniques.

publications.title: Memory-Efficient Native-Resolution Training of U-Net for UAV-Based Weed Segmentation in Sugar Beet Fields
publications.venue: Smart Agricultural Technology (Elsevier) — manuscript in submission
publications.details: Proposes a hybrid memory-optimization framework combining selective activation checkpointing with CPU offloading, enabling full-resolution UAV image training on a single H100 GPU.

projects.title: PDF-Grounded RAG Chat Application
projects.technologies: Python, FastMCP, FAISS, LLM APIs, Claude Code
details: Built an end-to-end RAG pipeline from scratch — PDF parsing, FAISS vector indexing, transformer embeddings, and LLM API integration (Claude, GPT); applied prompt engineering to minimize hallucination and evaluated output quality against factual grounding metrics.

Exposed the pipeline as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server using Anthropic's FastMCP SDK, with index_pdf and ask_document as callable tools; integrated with Claude Code for natural-language querying of local PDFs, reducing manual document review time by 60%.

projects.title: Edge-Deployable Speckle Sensing via Knowledge Distillation
details: Designed a teacher–student knowledge distillation framework (KL divergence + cross-entropy) compressing SqueezeNet (724k params) into TinyCNN (97k params, 0.37 MB); distilled student achieved 100% validation accuracy at 7× compression with zero additional inference cost.

projects.title: BuilderSoft.ca Full-Stack Workflow Automation
details: Owned end-to-end delivery: designed MySQL schemas, built FastAPI and GraphQL APIs with role-based access control, and automated work-log tracking, task management, and invoicing workflows deployed and documented for team handoff.

projects.title: Credit Card Fraud Detection
details: Built an end-to-end ML pipeline on the IEEE-CIS dataset: automated data ingestion, feature engineering, and preprocessing; trained XGBoost and autoencoder models to detect fraudulent transactions at scale using anomaly detection.

Evaluated models using ROC-AUC and feature importance analysis; benchmarked multiple approaches to identify the best performing configuration, establishing a reusable, documented experiment framework.

projects.title: Image Captioning System
details: Built an image captioning system using Inception V3 for visual feature extraction and a transformer-based decoder for caption generation; trained and evaluated on the MSCOCO 2017 dataset.

activities: Organized 'Kshitiz,' an event featuring IT alumni sharing professional experiences.
Collaborated with the Open Tech Community to host a hackfest event.
Mentored 15+ club members through regular meetings and feedback.
Led recruitment by interviewing and onboarding new members.
Conducted a GitHub workshop with 80% active participation.
Led recruitment, onboarding 10+ new club members.
Volunteered for the 'Art of Living' course, engaging in team-building activities.
Volunteered for the 'Warm Winter' campaign, collecting clothes for remote areas.
